title: carex × enshuensis
intro: Carex × enshuensis, also known as Enshu sedge or Enshu grass, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Carex enshiensis and Carex lanceolata, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in China and Japan, where its rhizomes possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Carex × enshuensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Carex × enshuensis is commonly prepared in herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional remedies and tonics to promote overall well-being and health.
